english_source_text: Thousands of people gathered at La Casa Rosada to bid farewell to their idol. Three individuals hired by the funeral home responsible for preparing the Argentine star's body for viewing took several photographs next to the coffin. In two images, the three men can be seen posing beside the open casket, two of them giving a thumbs up, apparently in a sign of approval. The photographs ended up being shared on social media while Maradona was being mourned at La Casa Rosada, the presidential palace, provoking anger and outrage among many Argentines. 'Widespread rejection,' noted the country's major newspapers. Matías Morla, the agent and lawyer of the football legend, called one of the individuals who took the photo a 'scoundrel' and warned that he would take action over the incident. 'For the memory of my friend, I will not rest until he pays for such an aberration,' he said on Twitter, where criticism against those involved has accumulated, but some comments also justify their actions due to the fanaticism surrounding 'the Ten.' One of the individuals involved, however, has already publicly apologized and stated that he has received death threats over the situation. 'I apologize.' According to Claudio Fernández, the idea of taking a photo next to the open casket, accompanied by his son, was 'something...
english_translation: This year we have heard about gravitational waves, been alarmed by the Zika virus, and learned about the rocky exoplanet Proxima b.

english_source_text: Rondón became the first Venezuelan to score a hat-trick in England. The star of the Vinotinto received praise from the sports press in the UK on Thursday after scoring three goals in 13 minutes, making him the first player from his country to achieve the prestigious hat-trick in English football. To top it off, he scored all three with his head, becoming only the second player in Premier League history to accomplish this feat. The other was Duncan Ferguson in 1997. With his three headed goals, Rondón now has seven for the season. Analysts highlight that in addition to his finishing ability, he works hard on the field, a crucial task for a team that usually fights to avoid relegation each year but has transformed into a major revelation this season. 'Three perfect headers. The power, the precision, was simply fantastic tonight.' WBA is in seventh place in the table behind Manchester United, although they have scored more goals than the team managed by Portuguese José Mourinho. 'Three perfect headers. The power, the precision, was simply fantastic tonight,' acknowledged English football legend Alan Shearer, whose comments often resonate with fans. Since arriving at the central England team from Zenit in San...
english_translation: Salomón Rondón is experiencing his best moment in English football.

english_source_text: A can of tomato soup contains no less than 6 teaspoons of salt. And if you think that counting the teaspoons you add to your morning coffee plus some sweet treat during the day is enough, as we show in this video, you are mistaken. How much added sugar is hidden in savory food? Processed savory food also contains added sugar, and sometimes a single dish can exceed the recommended amount for the entire day. One of the challenges in controlling sugar consumption is detecting it on food nutrition labels, as it can appear under different names. For example, the new nutrition information labels approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. According to the National Health Service of the United Kingdom, there are more than a dozen names that refer to sugar, such as fructose, glucose, sucrose, and molasses. To prevent consumers from having to take advanced chemistry courses to understand food nutrition information, the U.S. Food and Drug Administration announced in May that the label format will be modified in the country. Starting in July 2018, added sugar will be shown separately and expressed in grams and as a percentage based on a 2,000-calorie diet. These are the...
english_translation: The World Health Organization (WHO) recommends that added sugar consumption should not exceed 10% of daily caloric intake.

english_translation: The death of five police officers in Dallas (Texas) this Thursday in an attack carried out by snipers is the deadliest event for police in the United States since the September 11, 2001 attacks.

english_source_text: Despite centuries of domestication, European strawberries remained a small delicacy in 1711. However, in a region between the Andes and the Pacific, indigenous people had been cultivating and enhancing them for nearly a thousand years. Julius de Berry from Antwerp had gifted a bowl full of ripe wild strawberries; as a reward, the king named the family 'Fraise' (strawberry in French). 800 years later, another king of France, Louis XIV, was worried because his country was not faring well in the War of Spanish Succession and feared that a peace treaty would leave the court of Versailles without access to the riches of America. In 1711, he decided to send a spy to the south of the New World to gather information. The chosen one turned out to be none other than Amedée François Frézier. The surname had changed slightly, as the family had settled in Scotland in the Middle Ages, as part of the entourage of the French ambassador, and there it became Frazer; upon returning to France, it became Frézier, but Amedée was a descendant of Julius, who had gifted strawberries to Charles 'the Simple'. In one of those curious coincidences of history, his journey to South America would forever change that delicacy that so pleased the monarch.
english_translation: When in 1364 King Charles V of France received a gift from his cousin, King Charles IV of Luxembourg, he was delighted.

english_source_text: Statistics show a reduction in attacks. But the threat continues. Potts told the BBC that several factors led to Somali pirates being 'contained and restrained' and that a joint naval forces operation at one of their land bases last May had a profound effect and is deterring pirates from going to sea. However, the rear admiral also warned that progress against piracy is weak and still reversible. Statistics reveal a dramatic reduction in piracy attacks off the Somali coast. In 2011, according to EU Navfor Somalia, there were 151 verified attacks on sizable ships, and 25 vessels were successfully pirated. So far this year, the number of attacks has dropped to 31, and only five ships have been captured, the last one in May. 'October and November would normally be two of the most active months for maritime piracy as they fall between the two monsoon seasons,' says Potts. 'But this year they have been surprisingly quiet.' The source of the problem is not at sea, but on land. Potts lists four factors for the reduction in piracy. First is the use of private security guards on ships...
english_translation: British Rear Admiral Philip Potts, commander of the European Union naval force in Somalia, says piracy in the waters off the Horn of Africa is at its lowest point in five years.

english_source_text: Guillermo Del Toro dedicated his award to young Latin American directors. The Mexican director said that the coveted award was a confirmation that one should hold on to the things they believe in. "In my case, monsters," he noted. Del Toro's latest film tells the story of a cleaning lady (Sally Hawkins) who falls in love with an aquatic creature. The film "Foxtrot," by Israeli director Samuel Maoz, won the Silver Lion of the Grand Jury. French director Xavier Legrand won the Silver Lion for Best Director for his drama Jusqu'à la Garde (Custody). Del Toro dedicated his award to young Latin American directors. "As a Mexican, I dedicate this award to all Mexicans and Latin American directors who dream of doing something like a parable, to those who are told it cannot be done. Yes, it can be done," he said.
english_translation: Mexican Guillermo Del Toro won this award for young Latin American directors.

english_source_text: Peña Nieto: "Mexico will not pay for any wall" With these words, in a recorded video message, the President of Mexico, Enrique Peña Nieto, responded to the executive order issued this Wednesday by U.S. President Donald Trump to expand a wall on the border between the two countries. "Mexico does not believe in walls. I have said it time and again: Mexico will not pay for any wall," Peña Nieto assured. The Mexican president did not clarify whether he would cancel the meeting with Trump in Washington, scheduled for January 31 at the White House. Legislators and political party leaders have requested this since Tuesday night when Trump announced the construction of the wall on his Twitter account. Peña Nieto stated that he would decide "the next steps" after consulting with the Senate, state governors, and the final report from Foreign Secretary Luis Videgaray and Economy Secretary Ildefonso Guajardo. The officials met at the White House with part of the U.S. president's team this Wednesday. The meeting lasted about 8 hours. However, in an interview with Televisa, Videgaray said that the meeting between Trump and Peña Nieto is still on. "The working meeting between the presidents next Tuesday is still confirmed. The meeting is still on, confirmed," he insisted. However, to increase pressure, this Thursday the president...
english_translation: "We demand respect."

english_source_text: The new bridge features the latest technology. The new bridge is called San Giorgio, made of steel and concrete, and was designed by the renowned Genoese architect Renzo Piano. The new structure boasts high technology. It has a dehumidification system and four maintenance robots responsible for detecting any type of erosion. The works were completed a year after the demolition of the remains of the previous bridge. "On one hand, I feel proud, but at the same time, this bridge is born from a tragedy. This will never be forgotten," said the 82-year-old architect to the BBC. Tragedy On August 14, 2018, after a heavy storm and intense rains, the Morandi bridge collapsed, and 210 meters of its structure and one of the support towers fell. Vehicles that were then crossing the bridge fell in a cascade, and several apartment blocks around were crushed. 210 meters of the Morandi bridge came down, causing vehicles to fall and buildings to be crushed. 43 people died. At the inauguration ceremony of the new bridge, the name of each victim will be read aloud as a form of tribute. However, some relatives of the deceased prefer not to attend. "We will not be at the inauguration; we do not want the tragedy to turn into a carnival," said Egle Possett, whose sister...
english_translation: The new bridge in Genoa, which replaces the one that collapsed two years ago, was inaugurated this Wednesday.

english_source_text: Kandovan, in Iran, is one of the most fascinating destinations shaped by nature: it looks like a giant termite colony of mud cones. But sometimes the most fascinating cities and towns are those shaped by nature, often with surprisingly surreal results. To find the most spectacular natural places where people actually live, we turned to the Q&A site Quora. Its users told us about caves carved into volcanic rock and cities built in meteorite craters, among other destinations that look nothing like the standard tourist brochure. Kandovan, Iran Since the 13th century, or even earlier, the residents of this village have carved their conical houses into the soft volcanic rock of the area, a legacy of the previous eruptions of the now inactive Mount Sahand volcano. Located 55km south of Tabriz, the village looks like a giant colony of mud cones filled with holes. In addition to having a surreal appearance, the troglodyte homes provide natural heating and cooling, says Quora user Kaushik Parashar from Guwahati, India. 'The hardened material provides efficient insulation for the summers and harsh winters,' he says. Travelers can also enjoy the benefits of these dwellings...
english_translation: When planning our vacations, we often focus on the most well-known cities and towns.

english_source_text: Each of its projectiles costs more than $800,000. In fact, it is the most expensive warship ever built by the U.S. Navy: it cost over $4 billion. Its naval engineering system is so advanced that it can hit targets more than 128 kilometers away. It is designed to be registered by radars as a simple fishing boat, thanks to its angular shape. "It is simply gigantic once it starts to approach," noted Lawrence Pye, a lobster fisherman whose radar detected a fishing boat of only 12 to 15 meters in length. However, the 14,500-ton vessel measures no less than 185 meters long. It is the jewel of the U.S. Navy, but the price of its ammunition is as exorbitant as its size. Its development began in the 1990s and it is the first of a "new generation of warships that must adapt to the current global political landscape," stated its manufacturer, Bath Iron Works, on its website. But it has a small (or rather, big) drawback: each of its projectiles costs more than $800,000. And now the Pentagon is considering that perhaps this weapon, as advanced as it is, is too costly. Exorbitant price. This October, during a presentation ceremony in Baltimore, U.S., Admiral Harry Harris, head of the Command...
english_translation: The most expensive warship ever built by the U.S. Navy, the USS Zumwalt, has a small (or rather, big) drawback: each of its projectiles costs more than $800,000.

english_source_text: At a press conference, Putin stated that the ousting of Ukrainian President Viktor Yanukovych was an 'unconstitutional coup and an armed takeover of power.' He also denied that Russian soldiers had besieged Ukrainians in the Crimean Peninsula, claiming that all forces there were 'pro-Russia self-defense forces.' Crimea has become the focal point of the crisis in Ukraine after a series of violent protests forced Yanukovych from power. Troops in apparently Russian uniforms surround Ukrainian military bases. Russia effectively controls the peninsula. Thus, the question that resonates is: does Russia have sufficient and legitimate reasons to intervene in Crimea? In BBC Mundo, we analyze Russia's justifications for a possible intervention in eastern Ukraine. Read: The (dis)balance of military power between Russia and Ukraine. What drives Russia to defend Crimea? Soviet leader Nikita Khrushchev handed Crimea to Ukraine in 1954. Historical connections to the peninsula date back to the times of Catherine the Great in the 18th century when Russia conquered southeastern Ukraine and the Crimean Peninsula from the Ottoman Empire. In 1954, Crimea was given to Ukraine as a gift from Soviet leader Nikita Khrushchev, who was half Ukrainian. Just ten years earlier, Joseph Stalin had...
english_translation: Russian President Vladimir Putin defended on Tuesday the decision to send troops to Ukraine, stating that his goal is to protect Russian citizens living there.

english_source_text: In recent months, state media have publicly celebrated several achievements in advanced technology. Or at least that's what they say. As with most things in that country, it's hard to verify what's true and what's not. Nevertheless, it is significant that they are giving importance to technology. Recently, state media have publicly celebrated various achievements in advanced technology, including a 'smart home system.' Beyond the purpose of propaganda, the emphasis on this sector reflects North Korea's intention to leverage technology to improve its economy, a key goal for Supreme Leader Kim Jong-un. One of the most recent ventures is a new wifi service called Mirae, which allows mobile services to access the state-approved intranet network in the capital, Pyongyang. State television showed in early November how an Airang 171 smartphone was used to access Mirae during the 'Exhibition of Technological Achievements.' The U.S.-based monitoring page 38North noted that this was the first time an outdoor wifi service was mentioned in North Korean media and it operates alongside two cellular networks that provide data connection services...
english_translation: The North Korean government announced this Monday that North Korea will undergo an industrial revolution.

english_source_text: Toyota has benefited from sales in the markets of Japan and the United States. The automotive giant predicts a net profit of $9.7 billion for the financial year ending March 31, 2013, thanks to a strong recovery in sales in Japan and the U.S. Toyota based its profit projection after recording a 221% increase in the quarter from July to September compared to the same period last year. "During the first six months of this fiscal year, we have seen a significant increase in production across all regions compared to the same period last year, when we suffered a shortage of parts supply," said Satoshi Ozawa, executive vice president of Toyota. However, the Japanese firm showed caution as its sales could be affected by the ongoing territorial dispute between Japan and China and the severe crisis affecting several countries in Europe.
english_translation: Toyota expects an increase in its profits due to a rise in sales in Japan and the United States.

english_source_text: In Brazil, it is practically a given that the Senate will suspend Rousseff for up to 180 days. The news comes just as it is considered almost certain that the Senate will suspend Rousseff next week to subject her to an impeachment trial. The request to open a judicial investigation against the president was sent by the attorney general to the Supreme Court confidentially and, according to reports, includes a request to investigate former president Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva. For the procedure to be opened, it requires the authorization of Teori Zavascki, the Supreme Court minister (judge) overseeing the processes related to the massive corruption scheme at Petrobras. The suspicions The prosecutor's request is based on suspicions that Rousseff attempted to appoint Lula as a minister in her government to give him special judicial status that would prevent his potential arrest due to the Petrobras scandal, the media reports. Judge Sérgio Moro disclosed in March a recording of a phone conversation where Rousseff informed Lula that she would send him the act of his appointment to use 'in case of necessity.' The president has denied that those words were related to the Petrobras case and stated that she sent the document because she did not know if Lula could attend the act of his appointment. Both Rousseff...
english_translation: Brazilian judge Sérgio Moro announced on Tuesday that President Dilma Rousseff will be suspended for the first time.

english_source_text: Kim Jong-un next to a computer resembling an Apple. The Red Star operating system was designed to superficially resemble Apple's OS X, but it has hidden features that allow it to tag files and link them to specific individuals. The hidden tools were discovered by Florian Grunow and Niklaus Schiess, two German researchers who conducted the analysis last month. Grunow and Schiess presented their findings on Sunday at the 'Chaos Communication Congress,' a conference held in Hamburg with experts in security, cryptography, privacy, and freedom of expression. The researchers thoroughly reviewed the code of the Red Star 3.0 operating system, which appeared online a year ago. As Grunow explained to the BBC, the system's developers 'did a very good job' copying the basic design and functionality of Apple computers. North Korea's previous operating system resembled Windows more. But they added a special feature: any file uploaded to the system with a USB drive or other storage device can be tagged, allowing the State to track its path from one machine to another. Red Star can also identify unwanted files and delete them without authorization. A very sophisticated system...
english_translation: North Korea's spy operating system, according to reports on Sunday, is designed to create a North Korean spy operating system.

english_source_text: Russia has been an indispensable ally for Bashar al-Assad's government in Syria. Currently, he is a professor and a member of the Institute for Public Diplomacy and Communication at George Washington University. The following article for the BBC reflects his personal opinion on Moscow's growing influence in seeking a solution to the Syrian crisis. Read: Why is Russia becoming more involved in the war in Syria? ----------------------------------- End of Perhaps you might also be interested It is difficult to get an accurate idea from the dramatic images of refugees and immigrants heading towards Europe that we have seen in recent weeks. Many of them in boats, others by train, and others on foot. Who are they? Why are they fleeing in seemingly increasing numbers? Why now? What has changed? After five years of civil war, the question is whether Syria will continue to exist as a single state. Europe is trying to provide a coherent response to the problem, taking into account the variety of capacities and political trends of its 28 members. From the compassionate pragmatism of German Chancellor Angela Merkel to the angry populism of Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban. And these refugees and immigrants are fleeing from many conflicts and crises. But the main common factor seems to be the civil war in Syria,...
english_translation: American journalist Jonathan Marcus was one of the first journalists to arrive in Russia in 2011.

english_source_text: For experts, Ingvar Kamprad transformed the way home furniture is marketed (Photo courtesy: Ikea). Kamprad died "peacefully" at his home in Smaland, the statement noted. The company said that Kamprad was "one of the greatest entrepreneurs of the 20th century." Throughout his career, the founder of Ikea faced scrutiny over his past ties to the Nazis, which he called "the biggest mistake" of his life. He attributed his involvement to "youthful stupidity." "He worked until the end of his life, remaining true to his own motto: most homes are yet to be made," the company added. In 2013, Kamprad decided to step down from Ikea's board. The teenage investment The billionaire was born in 1926 in Smaland and founded Ikea when he was 17. He had used money his father gave him as a gift for his good grades in school, despite being dyslexic. Ingvar Kamprad founded IKEA in 1943. Here he is at a tribute with Princess Victoria of Sweden. "Ingvar Kamprad was a great entrepreneur typical of southern Sweden: very hardworking and stubborn, with much warmth and a playful sparkle in his eyes," the company wrote when announcing his passing. The designs created by his company became very popular partly due to their simplicity and low cost. Furniture design expert Jeff Banks indicated that the...
english_translation: The founder of Ikea, Ingvar Kamprad, became an icon of the modern economy.

english_source_text: The image of Abdul trying to sell pens moved many internet users. The man is Abdul Halim Attar, a Palestinian refugee from Yarmouk in Syria, and his daughter's name is Reem. We know this thanks to an Icelandic activist, Gissur Simonarson, who managed to locate both of them. Simonarson, who runs the news site Conflict News, posted the photo on his Twitter account after seeing it shared, and it quickly gained traction among his 6,000 followers. He then decided to search for the man and, in less than half an hour, with the help of local journalists and activists in Lebanon, he managed to track him down. Successful campaign Then, on Thursday, Simonarson opened a Twitter account and started a crowdfunding campaign through Indiegogo, aiming to raise $5,000 in 15 days for Abdul and his daughter. Less than half an hour after asking for help to find them, Simonarson located Abdul and his daughter Reem. The goal was reached in just 30 minutes. In 22 hours, it had surpassed $60,752 and the amount continues to rise. "My goal was to get him some help. I didn't expect the great support we are receiving now," Simonarson told the BBC. "Now it seems he could start a new life with this money." When Attar learned what had happened, he broke down in tears.
english_translation: The image of a man and a girl at a pen stand in the Lebanese city of Qasar moved many internet users.

english_source_text: The change in timbre was identified in mothers speaking ten different languages, including Spanish, English, Mandarin Chinese, and Hebrew. However, what scientists did not know until now is that they also unconsciously change the timbre. This characteristic is like the fingerprint of our voices. Timbre is what differentiates a piano from a guitar when they play the same note. It makes our voices sound more or less velvety, hoarse, rough, or nasal, and to some extent, it is determined by the physical characteristics of our larynx and vocal cords. Researchers at the Child Laboratory at Princeton University in the United States discovered that mothers unconsciously use a different timbre when speaking to adults compared to when they speak to their babies. This vocal change was identified in mothers speaking nine different languages besides English, including Spanish, Mandarin, and Hebrew. 'There is a consistent pattern among all mothers. They all use the same type of change to switch from one mode to another,' Dr. Elize Piazza, co-author of the study, told the BBC. 'Vocal fingerprints.' The researchers analyzed the speech patterns of 24 women, 12 native English speakers and 12 from other languages. The study's results may be useful in developing...
english_translation: Scientists knew that mothers change the tone and rhythm of their voice when speaking to their babies.

english_source_text: Soros: "We have to experiment and Uruguay is one of the countries experimenting." The name of the 83-year-old Hungarian-American resonated in the debate over cannabis in Uruguay, after it was revealed that Open Society funded a campaign in favor of the law that last week made that country the first to legalize the drug's trade. Soros, a controversial investor, speculator, and philanthropist who in 1992 contributed to triggering a devaluation of the pound sterling, also met in September with Uruguayan President José Mujica to discuss his initiative regarding marijuana. "As he has influence in some important NGOs, he has been collaborating and will continue to collaborate on that," Mujica stated at the end of that meeting in New York. Founded and chaired by Soros, Open Society also funds groups that promote changes in cannabis strategy in the region and the United States, including the Global Commission on Drug Policy, which includes former presidents Fernando Henrique Cardoso (Brazil), Ernesto Zedillo (Mexico), and César Gaviria (Colombia), among other figures. "Open Society has been a key player in supporting civil society groups that seek to promote an opening" in drug policy, John Walsh told BBC Mundo.
english_translation: In Uruguay, the United Nations anti-narcotics agency accused him of "interference." And in the United States, the conservative newspaper Washington Times links him to an alleged plan to "monopolize" the marijuana market.

english_source_text: Francisco Rodríguez contradicts other Venezuelan economists who oppose the electoral battle. Rodríguez, economic advisor to candidate Henri Falcón, has entered the campaign for the presidential elections in Venezuela on May 20, which is heating up despite much of the opposition calling for non-participation. Maduro, who seeks re-election despite the severe economic crisis of a country in hyperinflation, already has Falcón in his sights, who appears to be his main and almost only rival. Falcón, who was a Chavista, leads the voting intention in several polls and is trying to gather support and break with the abstentionism proposed by a significant part of the opposition, which does not believe the electoral process is clean or fair. Henri Falcón is ahead in the polls two months before the elections. Among the support he already has is that of Rodríguez, 48, who has left his position as chief economist at the Wall Street firm Torino Capital to advise the candidate on economic matters, the root of Venezuela's problems. With Rodríguez's entry into the scene, Maduro stirs up fears of the arrival of neoliberalism represented in the 1970s by economists known as the 'Chicago boys.' The socialist president claims that Rodríguez intends to privatize companies, 'end the economy...
english_translation: Nicolás Maduro has a new enemy: Francisco Rodríguez.

english_source_text: Guardiola has achieved enormous success with Bayern Munich and Barcelona. The former Barcelona coach has been advising the Japanese rugby team. The team's head coach, Eddie Jones, says he approached Guardiola for some advice ahead of the 2015 Rugby World Cup, where Japan will seek its first victory since 1991. 'He is the best football coach in the world, and one always learns from the best,' said the Australian Jones. He explained to the Japanese news agency Kyodo News, 'Rugby and football are very similar in that you always want to move the ball into open spaces, and Bayern Munich and his previous team, Barcelona, have played with the most fantastic passing style you've seen. The principles are exactly the same.'
english_translation: The 'Super Bayern' of Japan, former football coach José Guardiola, advised the Japanese rugby team.

english_source_text: Despite the firmness of the sentence he faces, former president Luis Inácio Lula da Silva remains free. However, in the case of Brazilian Luis Inácio Lula da Silva, that is still to be seen. The judicial ruling this Wednesday against Lula undoubtedly represents a severe setback for the 71-year-old man who presided over Brazil from 2003 to 2010 with enormous popularity and influence in the region. Federal judge Sérgio Moro determined that Lula received approximately $1.1 million in bribes from the construction company OAS, in the form of a remodeled triplex apartment on the coast of São Paulo. Furthermore, Moro—who leads the colossal corruption case involving the state oil company Petrobras—concluded that the leader and pre-candidate of the Workers' Party (PT) for the 2018 elections is barred from holding public office. So, has the political career of Lula come to an end? The federal judge Sérgio Moro determined that Lula received approximately $1.1 million in bribes from the construction company OAS, in the form of a remodeled triplex apartment on the coast of São Paulo. Not necessarily, different Brazilian analysts assert, observing the legal and political situation of the former president. 'We cannot yet say that it is the end of his political career,' warns Cristiano Noronha from the Arco Advice consultancy in Brasília, in dialogue with BBC Mundo. Condemned but...
english_translation: The fact that a former president of Brazil is sentenced to 9 and a half years in prison for corruption is a common occurrence in most countries around the world.

english_source_text: For every 10,000 people (who participated in the study) who consumed 21 grams per day of red and processed meat, 40 were diagnosed with colon cancer. This is the conclusion of a new study from the University of Oxford, funded by the NGO Cancer Research in the UK, which adds evidence about the harm caused by consuming red meat. But how great is the risk? How much is exactly too much red meat? And what makes it harmful? Risk After analyzing information from nearly half a million people over six years, researchers found that 2,609 people developed colorectal cancer. They estimate that consuming 3 slices of bacon per day instead of one can increase the risk of colorectal cancer by 20%. For every 10,000 people (who participated in the study) who consumed 21 grams per day of red and processed meat, 40 were diagnosed with colon cancer. The comparable figure for those who consumed 76 grams per day was 48. It is estimated that one of the proteins in red meat (the one that gives it its red color) can damage the intestine when processed. According to the National Health Service (NHS), 76 grams of cooked red meat is equivalent to a sirloin (the cut of meat from the lower back) of just over 250 grams.
english_translation: Red meat is one of the major sources of colon cancer risk.

english_source_text: It is believed that Snowden is still in the transit area of an airport in Moscow. Six of the countries he has applied to are Latin American: Bolivia, Brazil, Cuba, Ecuador, Nicaragua, and Venezuela. However, the president of the latter country assured the BBC on Tuesday that he has not received any request. "No one has asked us for it yet," he said. President of Ecuador, Rafael Correa, stated in an interview with the British newspaper The Guardian that his country is not considering granting political asylum to Snowden and will only do so if the fugitive steps on Ecuadorian territory. According to WikiLeaks, the other countries to which Snowden applied for asylum include Spain, Austria, China, Finland, France, Germany, Iceland, India, Italy, Ireland, the Netherlands, Norway (whose authorities confirmed receipt), Poland, and Switzerland. He had also applied to Russia, but the authorities of that country reported that Snowden later withdrew the request after a series of conditions imposed by Moscow, mainly that he stop leaking U.S. secret information. It is believed that Snowden is in the transit area of an airport in the capital of Russia.
english_translation: Fugitive Edward Snowden has requested political asylum from 21 countries, according to the newspaper WikiLeaks.

english_source_text: The change in timbre was identified in mothers speaking ten different languages, including Spanish, English, Mandarin Chinese, and Hebrew. However, what scientists did not know until now is that they also unconsciously change their timbre. This characteristic is like the fingerprint of our voices. Timbre is what differentiates a piano from a guitar when they play the same note. It makes our voices sound more or less velvety, hoarse, rough, or nasal, and to some extent, it is determined by the physical characteristics of our larynx and vocal cords. Researchers at the Child Laboratory at Princeton University in the United States discovered that mothers unconsciously use a different timbre when speaking to adults compared to when they speak to their babies. This vocal change was identified in mothers speaking nine different languages besides English, including Spanish, Mandarin, and Hebrew. "There is a consistent pattern among all mothers. They all use the same type of change to switch from one mode to another," Dr. Elize Piazza, co-author of the study, told the BBC. "Vocal fingerprints" The researchers analyzed the speech patterns of 24 women, 12 native English speakers and 12 from other languages. The results of the study may be useful in developing...
english_translation: A mother speaks to her babies, and to her babies, it is one of the most complex aspects of motherhood.

english_source_text: Premier League leader Chelsea faces the Turkish champion tonight in the first leg of their last 16 clash. Former Manchester City coach Mancini noted that Chelsea, who have won the Champions League and Europa League in the last two seasons, are the favorites to advance. Mancini said, "They have great players who are used to matches like this." He believes there is an 80% chance they will progress. Despite Jose Mourinho's claim that his team is too young to win major tournaments, Mancini is convinced Chelsea has the ability to win a trophy this season. The Mancini-Mourinho rivalry flared up earlier this week when Mancini claimed he contributed to Inter's Champions League victory. Mourinho took over Inter after Mancini and won the Champions League with the Italian team. Currently the manager of Chelsea, Mourinho rejected the notion that Mancini had a role in that success, stating, "Six of my eleven players were not in his squad. So his team would only play with five players in the tournament because I had only five players from his team." Mancini emphasizes that former Chelsea forward Didier Drogba and Dutch midfielder Wesley Sneijder are crucial for Galatasaray's hopes of reaching the last eight for the second consecutive season. Tonight's match will not be Drogba's first against his former boss Mourinho.
english_translation: After Chelsea's victory in the Europa League, they are expected to win the Champions League.

english_source_text: A surprise awaits me in the elevator I take to go outside. I see a small piece of paper carefully attached next to the mirror in the elevator. At first, I think it's a lost notice or a for sale/rent sign. But upon closer inspection, I realize that the lines on this paper, prepared with a computer printout, form a poem. It's a poem about leaving one's home, with verses describing the journey in the elevator. By the time I reach the ground floor, the poem has ended. I catch a glimpse of myself in the elevator mirror, smiling under the influence of the poem, which also has a humorous side. I think, 'How strange.' Who put this poem here and why? Who wanted to send what message to whom? I turn the corner and head towards the park right in front of our house. A cool, yet sweet breeze is blowing. Although the trees have not yet blossomed, I can smell the fresh earth and spring as I walk along the winding path with yellow stones in the park. The trees on both sides signal that nature is waking up. When I reach the junction with a fountain where birds come to cool off in the summer, my eyes catch a piece of paper pinned to the trunk of a sycamore tree next to a colorful gazebo. Yes, this is also a poem. And this time, the lines are familiar, written in a pearl-like handwriting. It's a poem by the famous Hungarian poet Endre Ady about nature. As I read the poem,...
english_translation: I wake up early in the morning. The sky is still dark.

english_source_text: In expensive perfumes, Beirut residents sit on verandas sipping their cocktails until dawn, dressed in designer clothes. The city's liberal nature truly does not resemble the region's extremely conservative structure in any way. However, there is another aspect that highlights Beirut's uniqueness: its secret yet vibrant gay life. There are special bars and clubs for homosexuals in the country. These cautious venues do not welcome the media if there are undercover agents inside. 'Obscene images.' However, recent events seem to tarnish Lebanon's reputation, which has been relatively understanding towards gays, lesbians, and transgender individuals. Last year, a cinema in Beirut was raided by police, and over 30 people believed to be gay were detained. To determine whether they had engaged in 'natural' sexual relations, those detained were subjected to anal examinations at the police station. In June, psychiatrist Dr. Nabil Huri stated on one of the country's most-watched television channels that homosexuality is a disease that needs to be treated. Four months later, the screening of a French film depicting a love story between a gay couple was censored at the Beirut Film Festival. The Ministry of Interior deemed the film obscene due to scenes of two men kissing, the appearance of naked men, and sexual relations between men. 'Degrading...'
english_translation: Isn't Lebanon tolerant towards homosexuals?

english_source_text: A large majority of the 2700 refugees who want to go to Austria, Germany, and other European countries reached Slovenia yesterday. Slovenian Prime Minister Miro Cerar stated that military units are on standby to cope with the influx of refugees. Cerar emphasized that they will continue to accept asylum seekers as long as Austria and Germany keep their borders open. Hungary announced that it closed its border with Croatia due to the failure of EU leaders to agree on a plan to stop the influx of refugees. The news also mentioned that Hungary had closed its border with Serbia, another route to Western Europe, last month. Throughout the day yesterday, hundreds of refugees in Croatia were transported by bus from the Serbian border to the Slovenian border. Most of the refugees made a weeks-long journey on foot through Greece, Macedonia, and Serbia to reach the Croatian border. After registering the refugees, Slovenian authorities arranged buses for them to cross into Austria. A UN spokesperson stated that most of the refugees in Slovenia are Syrian, Iraqi, and Afghan. Spokesperson Caroline van Buren said, "They are fleeing from war. They are escaping to save their lives. Unlike other countries, Slovenia had time to prepare. It’s not perfect, but things are working." Croatia is an EU member. However, it is not in the Schengen Area like Slovenia or Hungary.
english_translation: Slovenia opened its border to refugees after Hungary closed its border with Croatia last night.

english_source_text: It was announced that the weapon was purchased by a private collector who called the auction company by phone, paying three times the estimated price. The small rusted pistol was found in 1965 by a farmer living near the village of Auvers-sur-Oise, where van Gogh spent his last days on the outskirts of Paris. The Dutch painter shot himself in the chest with a pistol after walking to a field near this village on July 27, 1890. The force of the gun was so limited that it took days for him to die from the wounds he sustained. The end of the Twitter post stated, '1 Van Gogh Institute: This tragedy is being commercialized.' About 70 years after his death, a rusted pistol was found in the same field behind the chateau where he stayed. Experts assessed that the gun could be the same one due to its age of 50 to 80 years. However, it has not been proven whether the found gun is the one van Gogh used to take his own life. The Van Gogh Institute criticized the auctioning of the gun as van Gogh's 'suicide weapon,' stating that 'this tragedy, which deserves more respect, is being commercialized.' The auction company, Art Auction, noted that while it is not possible to confirm the guns match, the overlapping dates strengthen theories in that direction.
english_translation: The pistol believed to be the one used by the famous Dutch painter Vincent van Gogh to commit suicide in 1890 was sold for 162,500 euros at an auction held in Paris, France.

english_source_text: Netflix announced that it has halted the production of the House of Cards series. Mexican actor Roberto Cavazos, who has appeared in many productions at London's Old Vic theatre, stated that Spacey continuously harassed young male actors during his tenure as the artistic director of the theatre from 2004 to 2015. In a note posted on Facebook, Cavazos said, "Being a man under 30 was enough for Spacey to feel entitled to touch us." On Sunday, Kevin Spacey was accused of harassment by American actor Anthony Rapp. Rapp, known for his role in Star Trek: Discovery, claimed that he was harassed by Spacey, who was 26 at the time, at a party in 1986 when Rapp was 14 years old. Spacey, who also directed House of Cards, quickly released a statement regarding Rapp's allegations, stating that he did not remember the incident but sincerely apologized for his "inappropriate drunken behavior" as described. In the same statement, Spacey came out as gay, but this announcement was criticized as it was made in response to the harassment allegation. Following the claims and the resulting outrage, Netflix announced that it has suspended the production of the sixth season of House of Cards, in a joint statement with production company Media Rights Capital, describing the decision to suspend the production as "the situation..."
english_translation: Following harassment allegations against American actor Kevin Spacey, the filming of the House of Cards series, in which he starred, has been halted.

english_source_text: The cars purchased by the Dutch police can reach a speed of 100 kilometers in 5.5 seconds. To intervene more effectively with criminals, the Dutch police decided to establish a fleet of 'rapid intervention vehicles' (HMA) some time ago. For this purpose, an agreement was made with the Germany-based Audi company, and 70 Audi A6 3.0 TDI model cars were purchased. However, De Telegraaf, one of the leading newspapers in the Netherlands, documented that 40 of these vehicles are currently being stored. This is because there is not enough trained personnel who have received special driving training to operate these cars. According to the newspaper, police officers who will use these cars, which can reach 100 kilometers in 5.5 seconds, need to undergo special training. However, the police academy is unable to meet the demands for special driving training. In over a year, only a small number of personnel have been trained in advanced driving techniques. Since basic driving courses and training for arrest teams are conducted together at the academy, the HMA fleet will remain without drivers for a while longer. According to the newspaper, the 40 cars, worth approximately 5 million euros, will remain in storage for at least another year due to the lack of drivers. Following the media coverage of the news, former Formula 1 driver Guido van der Garde offered to assist police officers with advanced driving techniques if needed.
english_translation: The Dutch police are forced to store their luxury cars worth 5 million euros because they cannot find drivers to operate them.

english_source_text: After the meeting, CHP Chairman Kemal Kılıçdaroğlu criticized the RTÜK's decision to suspend the program and impose a fine on Habertürk television due to Mersin Deputy Ali Mahir Başarır's controversial remarks about the Turkish army, saying, "This must be the legal reform they are talking about." He emphasized once again the importance of "friends": "Together with our friends, we will bring democracy to this beautiful country." The recent visits among opposition parties continued with Kılıçdaroğlu's visit to his coalition partner, Democratic Party Chairman Gültekin Uysal. The increasing meeting traffic among opposition parties is interpreted in political circles as a message to keep the "dialogue door open" for future alliances. A senior CHP official expressed the significance of Kılıçdaroğlu's visit with the following words: "We are visiting our friends. Ultimately, the components of democracy and those seeking justice in this country will come together. Turkey will evaluate those in favor of democracy against those in favor of a single leader. The Chairman is visiting all parties that demand democracy and justice." Kılıçdaroğlu, after the meeting with Gültekin Uysal, also made a statement to journalists regarding his party's Mersin Deputy Ali Mahir Başarır's remarks about Habertürk...
english_translation: CHP Chairman Kemal Kılıçdaroğlu visited his coalition partner, Democratic Party Chairman Gültekin Uysal.

english_source_text: Premier League leader Chelsea faces the Turkish champion tonight in the first leg of their last 16 clash. Former Manchester City coach Mancini noted that Chelsea, who have won the Champions League and Europa League in the last two seasons, are the favorites to advance. Mancini stated, "They have great players who are used to matches like this." He believes there is an 80% chance they will progress. Despite Jose Mourinho's claim that his team is too young to win major tournaments, Mancini is convinced Chelsea has the ability to win a trophy this season. The Mancini-Mourinho rivalry flared up earlier this week when Mancini claimed he contributed to Inter's Champions League victory. Mourinho took over Inter after Mancini and won the Champions League with the Italian team. Currently the manager of Chelsea, Mourinho rejected the notion that Mancini had a role in that success, stating, "Six of my eleven players were not in his squad. So his team would only play with five players in the tournament because I had only five players from his team." Mancini emphasized that former Chelsea forward Didier Drogba and Dutch midfielder Wesley Sneijder are crucial for Galatasaray's hopes of reaching the last eight for the second consecutive season. Tonight's match will not be Drogba's first against his former boss Mourinho.
english_translation: Galatasaray's coach Roberto Mancini acknowledged that Chelsea is a better team than them in the Champions League.

english_source_text: The symbolic clock is 2 minutes away from "striking midnight." The "Doomsday Clock" is updated by scientists who are members of the Bulletin of the Atomic Scientists (BAS). Updated since 1947, the clock serves as a metaphor for how close humanity is to destroying the world. According to scientists, the world has become an increasingly "dangerous" place, and it has never been this close to "doomsday" since 1953. In 1953, after both the U.S. and Russia tested hydrogen bombs, the clock was moved forward again, marking 2 minutes to midnight. Last year, it was also moved forward by 30 seconds. What is the reason for this decision? The committee announcing the decision in Washington, D.C. stated that it was not an easy decision and that there is no single reason behind it. BAS President Rachel Bronson said that this year's discussions centered around nuclear issues. North Korea's nuclear tests, rising tensions in Korea, and the verbal war between the U.S. and North Korea have been the most concerning topics for scientists. BAS also noted that the U.S. strategy to increase nuclear spending is alarming. The rising tensions between Russia and the West were also factors influencing the decision. Scientists say that when the clock strikes midnight, it will signal the end of the world due to global threats like climate change and the struggles of international institutions fighting against them.
english_translation: The committee announcing the decision in Washington, D.C. stated that it was not an easy decision and that there is no single reason behind it. BAS President Rachel Bronson said that this year's discussions centered around nuclear issues.

english_source_text: An examination of brain scans of 638 individuals who have surpassed retirement age revealed that those who were physically active over a three-year period experienced less brain shrinkage. Scientists noted that these exercises do not need to be heavy or intense; walking a few times a week is sufficient. Another striking point in the study was that playing chess and meeting with friends did not show a direct contribution to preserving brain volume. Researchers found that when examining the white matter in the brain, the damage rate in individuals who exercised at age 70 was lower compared to those who did not. It was also discovered that the gray matter, which is the source of messages in the brain, was higher in those who exercised. Experts already knew that the human brain shrinks with age, and it is also known that this shrinkage leads to a decline in memory and cognitive power. Previous studies have shown that exercise reduces the risk of dementia. However, scientists have not yet been able to determine how exercise helps reduce brain shrinkage and the risk of dementia. It is also known that exercise increases blood circulation in the brain, along with the rest of the body. A study conducted by the Alzheimer's Research organization based in the UK found that exercise...
english_translation: A study conducted in the UK revealed that a lack of exercise prevents brain shrinkage.

english_source_text: After his candidacy was announced, Demirtaş held a press conference and stated, "We will show during the campaign that we are not candidates for Çankaya alone, as individuals." He continued, "I do not possess any special talent or power. I was nominated simply because a candidate needed to be shown." Demirtaş mentioned that they are asserting the claim of the oppressed, the marginalized, and the masses to govern themselves, stating that with his candidacy, they have already "won for the people." He also expressed that the biggest deficiency in the presidential election is the absence of a female candidate among the nominees. For the presidential election, which will be put to a public vote for the first time in August, the Republican People's Party (CHP) and the Nationalist Movement Party (MHP) submitted their application for their joint candidate, Ekmeleddin İhsanoğlu, to the Speaker of the Grand National Assembly, Cemil Çiçek, on Sunday. President Abdullah Gül also announced yesterday that he would not run for Çankaya Köşkü again. The AKP candidate is expected to be announced on July 1. Deputy Prime Minister Bülent Arınç stated last Wednesday that "their candidate is largely Prime Minister Recep Tayyip Erdoğan." Arınç added, "If [the Prime Minister] is also willing, we will make this announcement on July 1..."
english_translation: HDP's presidential candidate Selahattin Demirtaş was nominated regarding his candidacy.

english_source_text: Filiz Tutya is a 53-year-old textile worker who has been in the industry for 35 years, spending the last seven years at Bravo Tekstil. Recently, the name Bravo Tekstil became known in Turkey due to a campaign initiated by its workers. They attached labels to jackets, pants, sweaters, and shirts in many Zara stores in Istanbul, stating: "I made the product you are about to buy, but I haven't received my payment! We produced for Zara for years. The factory closed overnight without paying our last three months' salaries and severance pay. Please tell Zara to pay us!" Thousands supported the #BravoIscileriIcinAdalet hashtag on social media. An online petition has gathered over 270,000 signatures so far. Bravo Tekstil went bankrupt and closed suddenly in July 2016, with the owner disappearing. The company produced for international brands like Zara, Next, and Mango. Workers, who claim they did not receive their salaries during the bankruptcy process, are now demanding Zara to pay their three months' salaries and severance pay. Filiz Tutya, who last worked as the Quality Technical Manager at Bravo Tekstil, says, "We had no other chance but this campaign: We will never..."
english_translation: Workers from a factory supplying Zara in Turkey say, "One day we came to the factory. One day we had a voice. But we have no employer. We are not receiving our payments. They should address our grievances."
