He emphasized once again the importance of "friends": "Together with our friends, we will bring democracy to this beautiful country." The recent visits among opposition parties continued with Kılıçdaroğlu's visit to his coalition partner, Democratic Party Chairman Gültekin Uysal. The increasing meeting traffic among opposition parties is interpreted in political circles as a message to keep the "dialogue door open" for future alliances. A senior CHP official expressed the significance of Kılıçdaroğlu's visit with the following words: "We are visiting our friends. Ultimately, the components of democracy and those seeking justice in this country will come together. Turkey will evaluate those in favor of democracy against those in favor of a single leader. The Chairman is visiting all parties that demand democracy and justice." The emphasis on "democracy with friends" was reiterated by Kılıçdaroğlu during a statement to journalists after the meeting with Gültekin Uysal regarding the remarks of his party's Mersin Deputy Ali Mahir Başarır about Habertürk...
english_translation: CHP Chairman Kemal Kılıçdaroğlu reacted to the sale of the tank pallet factory, one of the most critical periods in Turkish politics.

english_source_text: In the U.S., both the S&P 500 index and the Dow Jones index saw a 7% drop in the morning, leading to automatic trading halts for the second time this week for 15 minutes. Yesterday, the Dow Jones index had already dropped by 1,500 points, or 5.8%. In the first half of the day, the London Stock Exchange's FTSE 100 index recorded its largest drop in seven years with a 5.7% decline. The biggest losses on the London Stock Exchange were seen in travel companies, with IAG's shares down 9% and Tui losing 10%. Previously, there were significant drops in Asian markets, with the Tokyo Stock Exchange's Nikkei 225 index closing down 4.4%. In Asia, the Hong Kong Stock Exchange fell by 3.6%, and the Shanghai Stock Exchange dropped by 1.9%. Oil prices also fell by 4%, with Brent crude oil priced at $34.34. The cryptocurrency Bitcoin, which was trading at $7,859 last night, dropped 15% to $6,600 today. The effects of the coronavirus were also felt in Borsa Istanbul, where the BIST 100 index fell by 4.36% to 96,565.07 points in the first half of the day. The index had closed the previous day at 100,966 points with a 0.10% loss. While pharmaceutical companies saw a nearly 19% increase due to expectations of rising consumption, sports clubs' shares faced risks due to matches being played without spectators.
english_translation: In Europe, stock markets are experiencing declines following travel restrictions imposed by many countries due to the coronavirus and U.S. President Donald Trump's decision to halt entries from Europe to the U.S.

english_source_text: Are you tired, suffering from migraines, or feeling depressed? Then Isaac Pulvermacher's 'hydro-electric belt' might offer a solution! The device delivers an electric current to the relevant area through electrodes at both ends of the belt. Featured at the first World Fair in London in 1851, this device quickly reached annual sales of 50,000 units. Even the famous English writer Charles Dickens showed interest in this device. While suffering from severe leg pain during his reading tours in the 1860s, he purchased the electric belt in 1870 on a recommendation. Dickens likely used the electric belt in this way for his leg pain. According to medical historian Robert K Waits, 'when this belt was tested, the patient was not alive enough to report the result.' Indeed, Dickens's last letter was to inform the Pulvermacher company that he had received the device. Today, scientists resort to electrotherapy to alleviate certain ailments. Despite its fame, the Pulvermacher belt was not the first device to use electricity. In 48 BC, the doctor of Roman Emperor Claudius, Largus, attempted to treat migraines by placing an electric ray on the head. Although Largus may not have yet discovered the power of electricity, in the 18th century, scientists like Benjamin Franklin began to control this energy and understand its effects on the body. With the invention of the battery in the 19th century, this... [repetition continues]
english_translation: Can a person give themselves an electric current?

english_source_text: According to a report by Today newspaper published in the USA, Sierra Strangfeld learned that her baby had 'trisomy 18' syndrome at the 20th week of her pregnancy. However, Strangfeld and her husband decided not to terminate the pregnancy after learning that their baby would not live long after birth. 'We never thought about terminating the pregnancy.' Two months before the birth, as the risk of the baby dying became apparent, Strangfeld underwent a cesarean section. The baby, named Samuel, was born weighing 650 grams and died three hours later. Strangfeld, who has an 18-month-old daughter, said, 'We never thought about terminating the pregnancy. We wanted to welcome this precious gift, just like all babies, in the best way possible.' After Samuel's death, Strangfeld donated 14 kilos of breast milk to a milk bank called 'NICU.' Regarding her donation, Sierra Strangfeld said, 'We had nothing we could do to prevent Samuel's death. But there are things we can do for other babies,' and expressed her desire to establish a foundation for 'trisomy 18.' Trisomy 18 syndrome, also known as Edward Syndrome, is a chromosomal disorder caused by the presence of three copies of the 18th chromosome in cells. According to the UK National Health Service, 75% of babies with this syndrome do not survive more than 24 hours after birth. The percentage of babies living beyond one year with this syndrome is around five percent.
english_translation: Sierra Strangfeld donated breast milk that her baby had accumulated for 3 hours.

english_source_text: Cameramen capturing the conflicts in Syria from the Turkish border must ensure that the identification and interpretation of the facts are done in a way that does not favor any side of the conflict. In the UK, the BBC is considered a 'national broadcaster' by many, and it faces pressure to adopt a patriotic language in conflicts, using phrases like 'our soldiers' when referring to British troops and interpreting the war from a British perspective. However, what makes the BBC a reliable news source is its neutrality. Therefore, the BBC always uses terms like 'British forces', 'American soldiers', 'Pakistani army', and 'Turkish troops'. All broadcasters must show sensitivity, especially when discussing casualties. No news should endanger the safety of soldiers. There are also many anti-war individuals among viewers and readers, who know that certain military details will not be published until the possibility of affecting the conflict is over. However, if for any reason the news is censored or not fully conveyed, the reporter must clearly explain this and the reasons behind it. Known and unknown facts, as well as unanswered questions, should be honestly communicated to the audience. Casualties resulting from military actions, the numbers of those who lost their lives or were injured, or...
english_translation: As the conflicts in Syria escalate, anti-war protests are increasing in Turkey.

english_source_text: The news agency reported that Syrian forces have made contact with soldiers defending the airbase and have neutralized a large number of militants. The airbase had been under attack from ISIS for about two years. Meanwhile, in Latakia, the coastal city in western Syria that has been relatively unaffected by the country's conflicts, at least 22 people were killed in mortar attacks. Syrian state television reported that the mortars hitting residential areas injured 57 others. This was the largest attack to occur in the Alawite city, which is the hometown of President Bashar al-Assad and under the control of the Syrian army. Footage broadcasted by state television showed vehicles on fire and some vehicles hit by shrapnel. The Syrian army had launched a new operation against opposition groups in the towns surrounding Latakia, with Russian warplanes providing aerial support. No explanation has been given regarding which group carried out the mortar attack in Latakia, and no group has claimed responsibility yet. Reports are also coming in about attacks on the army near the capital, Damascus. The Associated Press reported that opposition groups have shelled some neighborhoods in Damascus, but...
english_translation: The Syrian army fulfilled the siege by ISIS, as the Syrian army completed its siege.

english_source_text: The actions are being carried out to protest layoffs and mandatory relocations in the public sector. On the first day of a five-day strike series, teachers went on strike today. Civil servants also called for a two-day strike on Wednesday. This strike is supported by universities, doctors, and lawyers. The layoffs are part of the bailout conditions imposed by the country's international creditors. These parties will meet in Athens to discuss the next loan installment. So far, Greece has received two aid packages totaling 240 billion euros. An additional 10 billion euros is needed to cover the funding gap. Strong resistance was met when a group of school security guards clashed with riot police outside the Ministry of Administrative Reform in Athens on Monday morning. Security forces used tear gas to disperse the protesters. Three people were hospitalized due to minor injuries. Officials from the European Commission, the European Central Bank, and the International Monetary Fund will gather in Athens this week to finalize the next 1 billion euro loan. In exchange for the loans, Prime Minister Antonis Samaras agreed to reduce the salaries of 25,000 civil servants this year before any transfers or layoffs. BBC reporter Mark Lowen in Athens notes that for the first time under the Greek constitution, public sector workers will lose their jobs. By the end of 2014, 15,000 public...
english_translation: Teachers in Greece have started a strike.

english_source_text: However, he did not provide details on who he met with from the US administration or how a program would be prepared. Mazlum Kobani stated that 'Joe Biden's election as President of the United States has given him greater hope for a realistic solution to the Syrian crisis' and noted that coordination with Russia continues, adding, 'It has always been challenging to maintain balance in our relations with Washington and Moscow.' Mazlum Abdi, also known as Mazlum Kobani, was a prominent figure during Turkey's Operation Peace Spring in the region controlled by the SDF in October 2019. Former US President Donald Trump thanked Kobani several times during and after the operation, which drew reactions in Ankara, where President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan stated, 'America must hand this man over to us.' McGurk frequently visited Syria during the Obama administration to meet with SDF-linked groups. Biden appointed Brett McGurk, the special representative for the anti-ISIS coalition during Barack Obama's presidency, as 'Coordinator for the Middle East and North Africa' at the White House. McGurk's decisive role in supporting the SDF in the fight against ISIS also faced backlash in Ankara. Turkey has reacted against the People's Protection Units (YPG), which form the basis of the SDF, due to their alleged connections with the PKK.
english_translation: Speaking to the UK-based news site al-Maghafi, Mazlum Kobani stated that a joint program is being prepared with the new US administration for the Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) and mentioned that the Trump administration had requested support for the SDF.

english_source_text: In an attack targeting a police convoy in the Essa Nagri area of Karachi, Chaudri Aslam Khan, who was at the forefront of the city's counter-terrorism operations, and at least two others were killed. Chaudri Aslam Khan had previously survived several assassination attempts. The Pakistani Taliban claimed responsibility for today's attack. A Taliban spokesperson stated that Khan was targeted for 'killing, torturing, and injuring Taliban members.' An explosive-laden vehicle collided with Khan's convoy. Photos from the scene show that many vehicles were completely destroyed, and nearby buildings were also damaged. Pakistan's Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif condemned the attack on security official Chaudri Aslam Khan. Reporters note that Khan is a well-known figure in Karachi. Since 2008, Khan's unit has led to the detention of dozens of militants planning attacks in Pakistan and Afghanistan, including those who could be suicide bombers.
english_translation: In a bomb attack in Karachi, Pakistan, one of the city's top security officials was killed.

english_source_text: Hurricane Matthew is the strongest hurricane to hit the Caribbean in the last 10 years. The United Nations stated that it could take days to understand the extent of the damage caused by the hurricane in Haiti. The death toll has exceeded 800. Carlos Veloso, head of the World Food Program, mentioned that some towns can only be reached by sea or air. The hurricane, which also affected Cuba, is moving towards the eastern coast of the United States via the Bahamas. A Haitian official speaking to the French news agency AFP reported that more than 50 people have died in the southern town of Roche-a-Bateau. It was noted that 80% of the houses in Jeremie, the largest city in the region, were completely destroyed. In Sud department, 30,000 homes were damaged. The photo shows Haitians carrying a coffin. Although the hurricane struck Haiti earlier in the week, the scale of the disaster is just emerging due to communication being cut off, especially in the southeast of the country. The storm's speed was reported to have reached 230 kilometers per hour over Haiti. BBC reporter Tony Brown in the area reports that Haitians are trying to repair their destroyed homes, and no aid has come from the government or military. UN: 350,000 people need humanitarian assistance. The United Nations announced that 350,000 people in the country need humanitarian aid. Reports are coming from the Bahamas of uprooted trees and power outages, but there is no information on casualties yet.
english_translation: The death toll in Haiti is rising due to Hurricane Matthew, which struck the Caribbean.

english_source_text: Today, some tribes that still practice hunting and gathering continue to remain naked. This shows that clothing is not essential for survival. So when did we start wearing clothes? We lack data to show when early humans stopped being naked and began covering their bodies with animal hides and skins, as clothing decays before it can fossilize. Instead, anthropologists try to determine the timeline through indirect methods. A study on lice conducted in 2011 revealed that the origins of clothing date back 170,000 years. Researchers believe that the human louse and the clothing louse diverged as species around that time. Without hair, there was clothing. At that time, the ancestor of modern humans, Homo sapiens, had emerged in Africa. They had less body hair. In contrast, earlier hominins were hairier. At a certain stage of their development, early humans felt the need to wear clothing. Some experts think that this need arose to compensate for hair loss. The minimal clothing worn by some tribes, like the Nuer in Sudan, who still practice hunting and gathering, suggests that covering up may not only be for protection but could also indicate the emergence of a sense of shame. However, there is no data to prove this. Other hunter-gatherer communities, like the Fuegian tribe in South America, also show that they sometimes wore simple clothing but also roamed naked.
english_translation: Clothing is an important part of human history. So, when did early humans start to wear clothes?

english_source_text: According to a Reuters report, Anton Moskvin, the deputy chairman of Rosatom, stated that the signing of the nuclear power plant agreement will take place by the end of the year. Speaking during his visit to the United Arab Emirates (UAE), Moskvin expressed that if the agreement is signed by the end of 2015, the construction of the first reactor of the nuclear plant to be established in the northern Egyptian city of Dabaa could also be completed by 2022. Moskvin said, "The sooner we sign, the sooner we finish," and added, "We can start the site assessment next year, and we will see how early we can begin construction on-site." Egypt has been planning to establish a plant in Dabaa, located in Matrouh province, since 1981. Following the Chernobyl disaster in 1986, Cairo decided to halt its nuclear program but announced in 2006 that it would revive it. Plans to tender the construction of the nuclear plant began after the ousting of former President Hosni Mubarak in 2011. Discussions are also ongoing with Saudi Arabia. In February of this year, President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi announced that a memorandum of understanding had been signed with Russia for the project. The Dabaa nuclear power plant, which is planned to have four reactors, is expected to be completed by 2025. Rosatom is the only company negotiating with Egypt for the nuclear project. Moskvin noted, "About 200 people from both sides meet every month to discuss commercial, technical, and other issues..."
english_translation: Discussions between Russia and Egypt regarding the nuclear power plant to be established in northern Egypt have reached the final stage.

english_source_text: Yenigün was first suspended from his school and then dismissed after signing the Academics for Peace declaration. He was in the United States, where he had been for a long time, giving conferences during the July 15 coup attempt. During this period, when passports were canceled and international departures were highly controlled, he canceled his short visit to Turkey and headed to the university in Germany that had accepted him. Yenigün, who received a scholarship in Islamic Studies at the Free University of Berlin, is now drawing attention to a changing situation. Years ago, Yenigün, who studied in Turkey and later completed his doctorate in the U.S. before returning to the country, states that Turkey used to have an appeal for those studying abroad to return, but this has now changed: 'Generally, citizens of other countries who go to the U.S. for their doctorate tend to stay there. Turkey had a special situation, a situation unique to academics, which was to return. Turkey was a country that could attract its citizens back, regardless of the circumstances. This situation has changed.' After the coup attempt, 15 universities were closed by decrees. The highest number of applications to the Scholar Rescue Fund, established by the International Institute of Education (IIE), came from Turkey, as noted by Sarah Willcox, the director of the fund, who participated in an event in September.
english_translation: Yenigün, an academic expelled from Harvard University in Germany, began returning to the country after signing the 'Academics for Peace' declaration, aimed at academics returning to Turkey after the July 15 coup attempt.

english_source_text: Many people reacting to the photo shared posts with the hashtag "We demand the arrest of Rebel Angel al-Shahri," calling for the young woman's arrest. Supporters compared the woman in her 20s to Rosa Parks, who was arrested in the U.S. for refusing to give up her seat to a white passenger on a bus. The woman, known as Melek al-Shahri and whose identity has not been disclosed, had shared a photo of herself wearing a jacket and colorful outfit, stating on Twitter that she had left her home without wearing an abaya or headscarf. According to Al-Sharq newspaper, the young woman was arrested after someone reported her photo. Security official Favaz al-Mayman stated in an interview with Al-Sharq that the woman is in prison for violating laws in the country. Mayman also mentioned that she is accused of "openly discussing forbidden relationships with men who are not relatives." Saudi Arabia has very strict dress codes for women. In a country where women are prohibited from going out without a headscarf and driving, they are also not allowed to be in public with men who are not relatives. Last July, Saudi women protested the control of men in their lives on Twitter with the hashtag "I am my own guardian," demanding a change in the "guardianship system."
english_translation: In England, a Saudi woman is accused of discussing forbidden relationships with men regarding black rights in the country.

english_source_text: It is stated that Obama's tax proposal could contribute an additional $320 billion to the treasury over 10 years, which could be used to finance tax cuts for other sectors. This speech, eagerly awaited by all segments in the U.S., is noted to potentially shape Obama's political legacy and set the economic agenda for the 2016 elections. However, Obama is facing opposition from Republicans, who hold the majority in both chambers of Congress. In his speech, Obama will emphasize that the benefits of economic growth should be reflected in ordinary families. According to American officials speaking on condition of anonymity, Obama's plan includes: - Closing legal loopholes that allow the wealthy to pass on certain assets tax-free - Increasing the wealth tax on the richest from 23.8% to 28% - Introducing new fees for financial companies with total assets exceeding $50 billion.
english_translation: U.S. President Barack Obama is expected to deliver a major tax reform speech this week.

english_source_text: In a study conducted by scientists from Imperial College London, 19 patients were given a single dose of a psychedelic substance called psilocybin. More than half of the patients reported feeling less depressed after taking the substance, and changes in brain function were observed. The positive effects of psilocybin on individuals with depression lasted for about 5 weeks. However, the research team warned that those suffering from depression should not attempt this method on their own. Previous studies had also shown that psilocybin could help individuals break free from the cycle of depressive symptoms by 'lubricating the mind.' The research team used fMRI (functional magnetic resonance imaging) to scan the patients' brains before and after the administration of psilocybin. The results, published in the journal Scientific Reports, revealed that psilocybin affected two key areas of the brain. Its structure resembles that of the 'happiness hormone' serotonin. The substance reduces the activity of the amygdala, a region that plays a significant role in processing emotions such as fear and anxiety. As the functions in this area decrease, so do the symptoms of depression. Additionally, psilocybin makes the brain's default-mode network, which is active when not focused on a specific task and consists of communication between different regions, more stable. The structure of psilocybin...
english_translation: Scientists suggest it could be used in depression treatment.

english_source_text: Following Turkey's downing of a Russian plane, Moscow initiated economic sanctions against Ankara. Russian Prime Minister Dmitry Medvedev instructed the freezing of joint investment projects with Turkey and stated on his Twitter account that "restrictions on Turkey could include financial and commercial transfers and bans on Turkish businesses." Medvedev also mentioned that the measures would include restrictions on food imports from Turkey. Russian Energy Minister Alexey Ulyukayev indicated that a range of measures, including restrictions on the TurkStream natural gas pipeline project, could be implemented. Shortly before Ulyukayev's statement, Prime Minister Medvedev had also ordered the preparation of a package of measures that included freezing joint projects. The Energy Minister added that the measures could also include limiting civil flights to and from Turkey and halting preparations for the establishment of a Free Trade Zone. The Russian Foreign Ministry also called on its citizens in Turkey to "return to their country." The ministry's statement noted, "In connection with the existing terrorist threats on Turkish soil, we once again urge Russian citizens to avoid visiting Turkey and for those currently there for personal visits to return..."
english_translation: The downed plane in Moscow suspended the red line.

english_source_text: The garden house in Hai Duong caused a stir in public opinion last May. Many news outlets reported that the son of Secretary Bui Thanh Quyen, Mr. Bui Thanh Tung, invested over 4 billion VND to acquire ownership of more than 4,000 square meters of land, including 500 square meters of aquaculture land converted to residential land without approval. Additionally, the Central Inspection Commission, which met from November 12-19 in Hanoi, stated that the use of 550 square meters of land for a water pond and rockery 'was not entirely in accordance with the intended use.' The Commission concluded: 'Upon review, there is no basis to conclude that comrade Bui Thanh Quyen intervened or directed relevant organizations or individuals in the procedures for transferring and changing land use for comrade Bui Thanh Tung...' 'However, comrade Bui Thanh Quyen needs to learn from this experience and seriously self-criticize for not regularly advising and educating his son to comply fully with land law regulations; not being decisive in allowing his son to buy and use land in Ninh Thanh, Ninh Giang, where some steps did not fully comply with regulations and procedures, causing negative public opinion and affecting personal and Party reputation.' The case of Mr. Bui Thanh Tung building a villa attracted public attention last May, and a National Assembly representative linked this issue to 'anti-corruption.' When the case appeared in the media, Mr. Bui Thanh Tung was serving as the Head of the Department...
english_translation: The Dong Tam case is considered one of the particularly serious incidents. After three days of trial, there were statements that drew public attention.

english_source_text: The 47-year-old coach has requested to terminate his contract, which was set to remain in effect until 2018. "It's not that I'm retiring; I haven't contacted any other clubs, but I have no plans to take a year off," he confirmed. Klopp has led Dortmund since 2008, guiding the team to two Bundesliga championships and reaching the Champions League final in 2012-13, but this season the team is currently in 10th place. They trail the top club, Bayern Munich, by 37 points. Klopp said at a press conference: "I truly believe this is the right decision. This club deserves to be coached by someone who is 100% accurate." "I have always said that when I believe I am no longer the perfect coach for this wonderful club, I will speak up." Regarded as one of the most talented coaches in Europe, the charismatic Klopp has led Dortmund to consecutive Bundesliga titles and became the first coach of Dortmund to achieve a domestic double. He also took the team to the Champions League final, a match at Wembley in England, where the team lost 2-1 to another German club, Bayern Munich. He is often mentioned as a candidate for clubs in the Premier League, including Manchester United before the team signed with him...
english_translation: Coach Jurgen Klopp will leave Borussia Dortmund at the end of the season. He has led the team since 2008, winning two Bundesliga titles and reaching the Champions League final in 2012-13, but this season the team is currently in 10th place, trailing the top team, Bayern Munich, by 37 points.

english_source_text: Lawyer Trịnh Văn Long stated that Mr. Trần has not eaten since being taken to Thổ Thành prison on Wednesday. He said his client wants to "protest the death of justice." Mr. Trần, who has not yet been formally charged, claims he is being punished by his successor for opposing closer ties with Beijing. However, this accusation has been denied by both the Chinese government and the current Taiwanese President Ma Ying-jeou of the Kuomintang. Mr. Trần has criticized the Kuomintang's China policy since leaving office in May after eight years as president. He was handcuffed and detained by prosecutors on Tuesday and then imprisoned on Wednesday. According to AP, the lawyer said Mr. Trần "opposes the authoritarian regime in Taiwan and the communist regime, and he wants sovereignty for Taiwan." The lawyer added that his client is currently in a "temporary stable" condition. Mr. Trần could be held in custody for a maximum of four months. He and his family have faced numerous corruption allegations since 2006, when his son-in-law was convicted of insider trading and sentenced to seven years in prison. These allegations have severely impacted the reputation of the DPP, now the opposition party, and could pose challenges for the party in next year's local elections. Taiwan separated from the mainland and has been led by the Kuomintang since 1949 after the civil war. The...
english_translation: Former Taiwanese President Trần Thủy Biển has gone on a hunger strike to protest the death of justice. Mr. Trần, who has not yet been formally charged, claims he is being punished by his successor for opposing closer ties with Beijing. However, this accusation has been denied by both the Chinese government and current Taiwanese President Ma Ying-jeou of the Kuomintang.

english_source_text: American scientists Mario R. Capecchi and Oliver Smithies, along with British partner Sir Martin J. Evans, made a groundbreaking discovery in the field of genetic research. This breakthrough led to a technology commonly known as 'targeted gene selection.' It allows researchers to inhibit certain genes, enabling them to understand the roles of these genes in health and various diseases. This method allows for the recreation of certain human diseases in mice by introducing genetic changes into the mouse genome. The Nobel Committee stated that this discovery has provided new insights into conditions such as cancer and heart disease. For instance, scientists can now understand how diseases can affect healthy individuals. This genetic research technology also helps in understanding the aging process in humans, as well as how a fetus develops in the womb. The Nobel Committee noted that this technology is now used across nearly all fields of biomedical research.
english_translation: This year's Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine was awarded to three scientists for their groundbreaking research in the field of genetics.

english_source_text: This is the first time the Pope has presided over such a meeting. The main focus of the meeting is the Eucharist, during which the issues the church is facing will be addressed. Among the current issues are the decreasing number of churchgoers and priests. A BBC reporter in Rome stated that the meeting may discuss relaxing the celibacy requirement to address this situation. Discussions will also focus on issues such as divorce and abortion. The strict control over discussions within the Catholic Church has been loosened compared to previous meetings, and this is the first conference where detailed discussions about the direction of the new Pope will take place.
english_translation: Pope Francis will preside over a special four-day church conference at the Vatican.

english_source_text: The discussion topic between Mr. Bush and President Lee Myung-Bak revolved around the process of denuclearization on the Korean Peninsula and promoting free trade. At least 20,000 security personnel were deployed to contain protesters, who had repeatedly taken to the streets to oppose a controversial beef import agreement. There was also a small anti-American protest when Mr. Bush arrived in Seoul on August 5. The U.S. President's planned visit to South Korea in July had to be canceled amid widespread protests. The beef import issue has led South Koreans to oppose the government's decision to resume U.S. beef imports after five years, as they believe this does not protect them from mad cow disease (BSE). In addition to this agreement, the two leaders are expected to discuss trade and the long-standing U.S.-South Korea military alliance. The two leaders had previously met in April, where they agreed on the controversial beef import deal. From South Korea, Mr. Bush will travel to Bangkok, Thailand, where he is expected to address the situation in Myanmar. He will then fly to Beijing to attend the opening ceremony of the 2008 Olympics.
english_translation: U.S. President George W. Bush met with his South Korean counterpart during his Asia trip.

english_source_text: The S-400 air defense missile system was showcased during the Victory Day parade in Moscow on May 9. He also added that Ankara will collaborate with Moscow to produce the S-500 defense system, according to Reuters. Turkey has requested China to close its detention camps. A U.S. Navy aircraft fell into the sea, and the F-35 fighter jets saw combat for the first time. China is purchasing Russian aircraft to enhance its air force. U.S. officials commented that Turkey's plan to buy the S-400 missile defense system is a 'big issue' and stated that this deal could risk Ankara's contract to purchase F-35 fighter jets from Lockheed Martin Corp. However, Erdogan mentioned in a discussion with students in Istanbul that Turkey conducted a technical assessment, and the results showed that the 'incompatibility between the S-400 and F-35' that the U.S. is concerned about does not exist. Now, they (the Americans) are showing reluctance. But sooner or later, we will receive the F-35. The (U.S.) not delivering the fighter jets is not an option,' Erdogan said. The trade war is slowing down China's aircraft carrier construction. The West is sending sample ships to the South China Sea to counter China. In August 2018, military experts noted that China's air force would be strengthened in both quantity and capability as a series of Russian fighter jets were delivered to Beijing. At that time, the Russian government confirmed that 10 heavy, long-range, multi-role Su-35 fighter jets would be delivered to Beijing.
english_translation: The U.S. added that China will produce the S-400 air defense missiles during the parade celebrating May 8.

english_source_text: Chinese President Xi Jinping met with Vietnamese President Truong Tan Sang in September in Beijing. The top leader of China will visit Vietnam from November 5 to 6. Both Chinese and Vietnamese media confirm that Xi Jinping will deliver a speech at the National Assembly of Vietnam on November 6. This seems to be an unprecedented event at the National Assembly of Vietnam. According to the schedule, upon arriving in Hanoi on November 5, Xi will hold talks with General Secretary Nguyen Phu Trong, witness the signing of several cooperation documents, and meet with Prime Minister Nguyen Tan Dung. On the morning of November 6, he will attend the 16th Vietnam-China Youth Friendship Meeting and meet with dignitaries from both countries. After visiting the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, he will speak at the National Assembly and then meet with President Truong Tan Sang. At a press conference last Friday, Li Jun, Assistant to the Head of the International Liaison Department of the Central Committee of China, said the visit would be 'short but fruitful.' 'The two countries will sign a series of cooperation documents in areas such as party cooperation, strategic development connectivity, infrastructure construction, economic-trade and investment, cultural education, and exchanges between the border provinces of the two countries...,' Li Jun said.
english_translation: Chinese President Xi Jinping will visit Vietnam on November 5-6. He will deliver a speech at the National Assembly of Vietnam on November 6.

english_source_text: The White House accused journalist Acosta of having "put his hands on" an intern during a press conference. CNN's White House correspondent Jim Acosta had his "hard pass," a press credential, revoked last week, just hours after he had a heated exchange with President Trump. CNN claimed this violated the constitutional rights of both the network and Acosta. A lawsuit, filed in Washington DC on Tuesday, November 12, names the president and his senior aides as defendants. Among those named are Chief of Staff John Kelly and White House Press Secretary Sarah Sanders, who stated that Acosta's unacceptable behavior was the reason for the revocation of his press credentials. On Tuesday, Sanders said in a statement that Acosta was one of nearly 50 CNN hard pass holders, adding that this was "not the first time this reporter has refused to yield the floor to other reporters." "The White House cannot have an orderly and fair press conference when a reporter acts in this way, which is both inappropriate and unprofessional," the statement read. President Donald Trump clashed with CNN reporter Jim Acosta at the press conference on November 7. "The First Amendment is not being upheld when a...
english_translation: A domestic journalist told the BBC that Donald Trump had "put his hands on" a young woman who was trying to do her job.

english_source_text: About 200 people participated in a protest against what they called the 'violation' of Iran's waters by 15 British Royal Navy sailors nine days ago. BBC reporter in Tehran, Frances Harrison, stated that these students are hardliners and they called on the Iranian government not to back down. The British Foreign Office said the embassy was not damaged and no one was injured. The protesters shouted slogans against Britain and demanded the expulsion of the British ambassador. The BBC reporter noted: 'They read a statement demanding the trial of the British sailors and insisted that the British government must apologize.' Riot police used tear gas to disperse the crowd and set up barriers outside the embassy to prevent people from jumping inside. However, as it is now the Iranian New Year holiday, many locals are unaware of this crisis involving the British sailors. Our reporter believes this is a worrying sign, and negotiations for the release of the British sailors will take time. The UK has clearly rejected claims that the British sailors were in Iranian waters when they were captured on March 23, while also calling for their immediate release. Defense Secretary Des Browne, currently in Afghanistan, stated that...
english_translation: Students supporting the UK protested outside the British embassy in Tehran during a violent demonstration.

english_source_text: Chairman of Ho Chi Minh City People's Committee, Nguyen Thanh Phong, stated that Thu Duc City, once established, will serve as the largest center for innovation and creativity in Vietnam. He also emphasized the desire for the central government to allow Thu Duc City to allocate budget for scientific research and technology activities. Thu Duc City, under Ho Chi Minh City, will be a hub for transferring new technologies to provinces in the Southeast region and expanding the development of technology services and products for the southern key economic region. Regarding the government structure, for the personnel structure of Thu Duc City in the future, Nguyen Thanh Phong proposed that after 5 years from the effective date of the National Assembly Standing Committee's resolution on the establishment of Thu Duc City, the drafting committee should study regulations limiting the number of vice presidents of this administrative unit to no more than 4 people. Additionally, the number of specialized agencies should not exceed 13 units, and the number of deputies should not exceed 39 people. Among the 13 agencies, depending on the actual situation, a Department of Science and Technology may be established, as the role of science and technology is crucial in the development of Thu Duc City within the context of a high-interaction innovative urban area. Based on this, Thu Duc City can propose to the local People's Council to establish other agencies suitable to the local situation. Ho Chi Minh City organized urban governance in 2021. Nguyen Van Nen: 'The issue of Thu Thiem will be resolved soon.' According to the draft...
english_translation: Thu Duc City will be the largest center for innovation and creativity in Vietnam, according to Chairman of Ho Chi Minh City People's Committee Nguyen Thanh Phong.

english_source_text: Abdul Hadi Awang declared at the annual PAS party conference that he accepts Anwar as a leader. Last September, Anwar's conviction was overturned, and he was released after serving six years in prison. Hadi's public support for Anwar Ibrahim is a clear indication that the former prime minister remains a key figure in Malaysian politics. The leader of the conservative Islamic party told supporters that Anwar's credibility could help expand their appeal. However, Hadi vowed not to abandon commitments to an Islamic state. Yet, this very commitment has previously caused rifts in opposition party alliances. Over 40% of Malaysians are non-Muslims and have abandoned parties that previously allied with PAS in past elections. Last year, even Muslim voters punished PAS for focusing on religion rather than everyday concerns like rural poverty and corruption. Anwar's persuasive ability may not be enough to unite the opposition and voters if PAS does not soften its stance. Currently, the former deputy prime minister has not responded to this call. Many here believe that Anwar Ibrahim still harbors ambitions for leadership...
english_translation: The leader of one of the main opposition parties in Malaysia has called for former deputy prime minister Anwar Ibrahim to lead a new coalition to overthrow the government.

english_source_text: Vietnamese Prime Minister Nguyen Xuan Phuc (right) and Chinese Premier Li Keqiang (left) in Beijing in 2016. The Ministry of Planning and Investment has warned the Vietnamese government about preferential loans and official development assistance from China. The ministry stated that many such projects are poorly managed while Hanoi has to spend more than expected, according to the Nikkei Asian Review. In a report sent to Prime Minister Nguyen Xuan Phuc, cited by Vietnamese media, the ministry noted that projects funded by Chinese loans generally have less favorable borrowing conditions compared to loans from other countries. According to VnExpress, the Ministry of Planning and Investment emphasized that the government should reconsider its direction towards borrowing preferential credit from China. Vietnamese reactions regarding train tickets printed with Chinese characters. Vietnam's economy is at risk if it relies too much on exports to China. The case of the nine-dash line T-shirt: 'Vietnam reserves its anger.' Loans and aid from China have an annual interest rate of 3%, higher than Japan's 0.4%-1.2%, South Korea's 0%-2%, and India's 1.75%. Additionally, borrowing from China incurs a commitment fee of 0.5% and a management fee of 0.5%, while the loan term and grace period are shorter than other capital markets, at 15 and 5 years respectively. Preferential credit loans are provided through the China Eximbank, according to VnExpress. Many projects under Beijing's ODA program are progressing slowly and of poor quality, leading to increased investment to address issues arising...
english_translation: While other countries like the U.S., Japan, South Korea, and India offer loans at low interest rates, China lends at higher rates. Many projects funded by Chinese loans progress slowly and are of poor quality, leading to increased investment to address arising issues.

english_source_text: Simon Cheng believes he would face danger if he returned to Hong Kong. Simon Cheng, a Hong Kong citizen, was detained for 15 days during a business trip to mainland China last August. Beijing accused him of inciting political unrest amid anti-government protests in Hong Kong. Cheng denied the allegations, telling the BBC that he was beaten and forced to sign false confessions while in custody. British government sources stated at the time that they found Cheng's claims credible. A pro-democracy advocate, Cheng expressed that returning to Hong Kong is too dangerous, fearing he could be arrested and taken back to mainland China again. In June, UK Foreign Secretary Dominic Raab stated that China had not provided a 'proper response' to the UK's inquiries about the incident. Cheng was granted asylum on June 26. In a press conference on Wednesday, he mentioned that he believes he is the first person in Hong Kong with a British National Overseas (BNO) passport to receive political asylum. 'I am grateful for the determination and courage of the British government in rescuing British citizens,' he wrote on Facebook. 'I also hope that the situation... [repetition continues]
english_translation: The UK has granted political asylum to a British consulate staff member in Hong Kong, who claims he was tortured in mainland China.

english_source_text: The prison on Nusakambangan Island. United Nations human rights officials have expressed concern over these executions, urging Jakarta to end 'unjust' death sentences. The identities of the prisoners have not been disclosed, but among them are citizens from Nigeria, Zimbabwe, Pakistan, and India. They are to be executed at the prison on Nusakambangan Island. The prisoners have been informed of this, according to Indonesian law, and could be executed early on July 29. Attorney General Muhammad Prasetyo stated that 14 death row inmates are in solitary confinement. The executions are expected to take place by July 31 at the latest. Zeid Ra'ad al-Hussein, the UN High Commissioner for Human Rights, expressed concern that the prisoners have not received fair trials and called on Indonesia to halt the executions. Relatives of the death row inmates for drug trafficking in Indonesia stated, 'The increasing abuse of the death penalty in Indonesia is alarming, and I urge Jakarta to immediately end these unjust sentences that are inconsistent with human rights.' He added, 'The death penalty is not an effective deterrent compared to other penalties and does not protect people from drug abuse.' Mr. Hussein called on Indonesia to restore the moratorium on the death penalty that was lifted three years ago. Relatives visited the prisoners on July 27 at Nusakambangan. Indonesian law is among the strictest in the world regarding drug crime penalties, and the execution of 14 drug traffickers - most...
english_translation: Prisoners in Indonesia were executed at the prison on Nusakambangan Island, while other prisoners were executed in Jakarta.

english_source_text: Colonel Le Trong Nghia, a victim in the recent 'Anti-Party Revisionism' case, passed away on February 22, 2015. The BBC and guests discussed whether to publicly reopen the case files of the 'Anti-Party Revisionism' case, which is believed to have many signs of 'wrongful conviction' and 'lack of clarity', following the death of a 'victim' from the case nearly half a century ago. This victim was Colonel Le Trong Nghia, former Director of the North Vietnamese Military Intelligence, and former secretary to General Vo Nguyen Giap. A document purported to be a draft 'Will' of Colonel Nghia, who died on Sunday, February 22, 2015, states that he requested the Party and Government, specifically the Politburo and the Party's Secretariat, to 'clear his name' and 'restore his honor'. He also asserted that he was not a 'spy' or 'traitor' but was implicated in the anti-Party revisionism case because he was a subordinate of General Giap. Several questions were raised by the BBC and guests in this week's discussion: What should be done about the anti-Party revisionism case? Is it time to reopen the files to understand the causes and motives of the incident, to examine whether this was a wrongful conviction, or even a case fabricated for political purposes? If this was entirely a 'fake' case, constructed with political intent, not just limited to 'internal struggles for power', but even related to 'foreign policy', relations with China, or foreign countries at that time of the Communist Party, then who...
english_translation: The BBC and guests discussed whether to publicly reopen the case files of the 'Anti-Party Revisionism' case, which is believed to have many signs of 'wrongful conviction' and 'lack of clarity', following the death of a 'victim' from the case nearly half a century ago.

english_source_text: At the first-instance trial on August 16, lawyer Dang Dinh Manh stated that the appeal trial was conducted quickly. Mr. Luong, just like at the first-instance trial, was 'always very calm, extremely composed, gentle, almost always smiling, although he firmly denied the charges.' Notably, the court added a penalty prohibiting him from holding positions in state agencies and armed forces for five years. Mr. Luong's wife said, 'My husband is being falsely accused.' Mr. Luong received a 20-year sentence, the highest for activists. Lawyer Dai commented that the trial of Mr. Luong 'still holds many mysteries.' 'The very content of the additional penalty indicates the regime's intention towards him. Even in 20 years, he will be over 70 years old, which is not suitable for holding state or elected positions, but perhaps they still do not want that possibility to occur,' Mr. Manh said. 'In Vinh city, Mr. Luong is not well-known, but in Yen Thanh district, he is like a spiritual leader. He has done many beneficial things for the people, fighting against the government over local injustices. And that may have made him a thorn in the side of the authorities.' Mr. Manh noted that at the appeal trial this morning, hundreds of people from Mr. Luong's parish gathered at a street corner because 'they thought that since it was a public trial, they would be allowed to attend.' However, only his wife...
english_translation: Some people from Vinh parish believe Mr. Luong is falsely accused and sentenced to imprisonment.

english_source_text: The suspect is of Uzbek origin. Mr. Vasip Sahin stated that the suspect, named Abdulkadir Masharipov, a citizen of Uzbekistan, is believed to have arrived in Turkey in January 2016. Mr. Sahin mentioned that the suspect has confessed to carrying out the attack and his fingerprints match those found at the scene. The Reina nightclub attack resulted in 39 deaths and dozens of injuries, with victims including citizens from France, Israel, Tunisia, Lebanon, India, Belgium, Jordan, and Saudi Arabia. The Islamic State (IS) claimed responsibility for the attack, stating it was retaliation for Turkey's involvement in Syria. The suspect was arrested by police on the evening of January 16 after a raid at a location in the Esenyurt suburb of Istanbul. Initial reports indicated that another Kyrgyzstani individual was detained; however, Mr. Sahin stated on Tuesday that an Iraqi man and three women of Egyptian and African descent were also held. Images of Abdulkadir Masharipov released shortly after his arrest showed him being held by a police officer, with a bruised face and multiple bloodstains. Mr. Sahin clarified that contrary to initial information, the suspect's four-year-old son was not present at the time of the arrest. The governor stated, 'clearly he [Masharipov] acted on behalf of Daesh to carry out the attack...'
english_translation: The Istanbul governor stated that the suspect arrested in the nightclub attack in Istanbul 'was trained in Afghanistan.'

english_source_text: The image of a coffin placed right at the entrance of the Pacific Crystal textile company in July. Behind it is a tent set up by the people of Hai Duong to protest. Meanwhile, local newspaper Hai Duong reported that authorities 'have done a good job of propaganda, mobilization, and explanation, so the clearance did not meet with public opposition.' Hundreds of residents in Lai Vu commune, Kim Thanh district, Hai Duong province have been setting up tents in front of the Lai Vu industrial zone for over 5 months, accusing the Hong Kong Pacific Crystal company of secretly discharging foul waste for more than two years. Some residents of Lai Vu told the BBC that for over 5 months, dozens of police and local authorities have continuously come to try to prevent them from setting up tents in the industrial zone; however, the peak of tension occurred on Monday afternoon, September 25. Reuters quoted a resident, Bui Van Nguyet, stating that 500 police 'used water cannons and electric batons to disperse about 200 residents.' The residents placed gasoline-soaked bottles to block the entrance to the Pacific Crystal textile company in protest against the company's waste discharge into the Lai Vu area. Another resident named Sim told the BBC that by around 3 PM, the residents were pushed out of the Lai Vu industrial zone, with two arrests and many injuries. 'We were very upset and went to the commune to demand the release of the detainees and take the injured to the hospital, but they...
english_translation: Hai Duong: 'Water cannons, electric batons disperse protests' -- The image of a coffin placed right at the entrance of the Pacific Crystal textile company in July. Behind it is a tent set up by the people of Hai Duong to protest.

english_source_text: Lopetegui has been coaching Spain since 2016. The events unfolded just two days before their first match in the World Cup against Portugal. BBC Game: Guess the 2018 World Cup champion. What are the chances for Asian teams at the 2018 World Cup? Fernando Hierro will temporarily lead the team at the World Cup. On Tuesday, European champions Real Madrid officially announced a three-year contract with coach Lopetegui following the resignation of his predecessor, Zinedine Zidane. Now, the Spanish Football Federation (RFEF) has announced that they have decided to dismiss the 51-year-old coach, stating that "RFEF did not receive any information" regarding the contract signing. RFEF President Luis Rubiales said, "I know this is a sensitive situation, and whatever decision I make will be criticized. But I assure you, this will make us stronger. I greatly admire Julen; he is a great coach, which makes my decision even harder." According to Spanish media, the president was very angry upon learning the news and interrupted a meeting at FIFA to fly to the team's headquarters in Russia to investigate the matter. Many team members, including captain Sergio Ramos, have advised the president to postpone the dismissal until after the World Cup to ensure the best state for the entire team. It seems Mr. Luis...
english_translation: Spain has sacked coach Julen Lopetegui just two days before the team enters the 2018 World Cup finals.

english_source_text: Goalkeeper Văn Lâm stood out in the quarterfinal match on January 24, where Ritsu Doan scored the only goal for Japan in the 57th minute. In the 24th minute, Japan scored from a corner kick, but referee Mohammed Abdulla Hassan Mohamed disallowed the goal after VAR indicated a handball by a Japanese player. Goalkeeper Đặng Văn Lâm made several crucial saves for Vietnam. However, Vietnam also had clear chances to score in the first half, forcing goalkeeper Shuichi Gonda to showcase his skills against Phan Văn Đức and Nguyễn Quang Hải. Sports commentator Quang Huy assessed the quarterfinal match against Japan. Ten minutes into the second half, VAR played a key role again by determining that Bùi Tiến Dũng committed a foul in the penalty area. Ritsu Doan scored the only goal, although goalkeeper Văn Lâm guessed the direction of the ball correctly. Despite their efforts, Vietnam ultimately could not overcome one of the tournament favorites, allowing Japan to advance to the semifinals. The first half was balanced. What transpired in the first 45 minutes might lead one to believe this was a battle between equals, rather than between a Japan that has won the tournament four times and Vietnam, which has only reached the quarterfinals for the second time, between a team that reached the Round of 16 in the 2018 World Cup and a footballing nation where the World Cup remains a distant dream. Japan had superior ball possession (68% to 31%), but Vietnam had more shots on target (3 to 2).
english_translation: Japan advanced to the semifinals after narrowly defeating Vietnam 1-0 at Al Maktoum Stadium in Dubai on January 24.

english_source_text: Mr. Nguyen Viet Dung, known on social media as Dung Phi Ho, has been detained and prosecuted under Article 245 for 'causing public disorder' since late April 2015. He was arrested after participating in a march to protect green trees in Hanoi on April 12. He was described as wearing a t-shirt with the emblem of the Republic of Vietnam and the English phrase meaning 'The people are not afraid of the government; only the government is afraid of the people' on the back. On October 7, lawyer Tran Thu Nam, one of three lawyers (along with lawyers Vo An Don and Le Van Luan) representing Mr. Dung, told the BBC that an investigation conclusion regarding the 'causing public disorder' case against Nguyen Viet Dung has been reached. 'However, since I have not been consulted on the investigation conclusion, I cannot comment on this case. I can only say that the trial will take place in the next two months. The sentence for Mr. Dung will depend on the trial's developments,' Mr. Nam said. 'He has matured and has his own ideals.' In a conversation with the BBC on October 7, Mr. Nguyen Viet Hung, Dung's father, shared that during the five months his son has been detained, the family has visited six times but has not been able to meet him, only sending supplies. 'I hope my son will receive a light sentence because Dung is still very young. I do not understand the law and what Dung has done, so I was angry at first. But later, I accepted it because Dung has matured and has his own ideals.'
english_translation: One of the three lawyers for Nguyen Viet Dung has been prosecuted for 'causing public disorder.'

english_source_text: In a meeting in 2014, Mr. Obama and Ms. Suu Kyi discussed the National League for Democracy (NLD) winning more than two-thirds of the seats needed to choose a president, ending decades of military rule. Ms. Suu Kyi's party gained control of both the upper and lower houses of Myanmar's parliament, according to the election commission. However, a quarter of the parliamentary seats were reserved for the military, meaning this faction would still have significant influence. Myanmar's constitution states that anyone with children holding foreign citizenship cannot hold the presidency. Ms. Suu Kyi, who has two sons with British citizenship, stated that she would ultimately lead the country. U.S. President Obama called Ms. Suu Kyi to congratulate her and her party, expressing hope that the election results would lead to a 'more peaceful and prosperous future.' Earlier, British Prime Minister David Cameron and U.S. Senator John Kerry issued statements praising the election process. Mr. Obama also directly acknowledged the 'success of the National League for Democracy' and praised Ms. Suu Kyi for her 'tireless efforts and sacrifices over many years to promote an inclusive, peaceful, and democratic Burma'—using another name for Myanmar in his commendation. Mr. Obama, who visited Myanmar twice during his presidency, expressed hope that the official election results would meet the expectations of all parties. The leader of Myanmar's military, General Min Aung Hlaing, stated that the military...
english_translation: The National League for Democracy (NLD) led by Aung San Suu Kyi has won the Myanmar election, according to results announced by the country's election commission.

english_source_text: This collection of wine was acquired during Jacques Chirac's time as mayor of Paris, featuring many rare bottles of champagne and Bordeaux valued at thousands of dollars. Chirac held the mayoral office from 1977 to 1995. The new socialist mayor has decided to sell all this wine to contribute to the public fund. French President Jacques Chirac was known for his refined taste, especially in gastronomy. While mayor, he often hosted lavish banquets. He and his wife spent about $600 daily on food and drinks. It’s no surprise that his wine cellar was filled with exquisite, expensive wines. Current Paris mayor Bertrand Delanoe ordered the sale of this wine after discovering many bottles were too valuable to be used for city banquets. The starting price for most of this wine is $750 per bottle, but some, like the 1990 Chateau Petrus, can reach $2,700. However, Chirac has since switched to drinking beer, so he may not be too upset about his wine cellar being liquidated.
english_translation: Mr. Chirac, the former French president, was the mayor of Paris from 1977 to 1995. He was a connoisseur, especially of wine. While mayor, he often hosted lavish parties. He and his wife spent $600 daily on food and drinks.

english_source_text: Mrs. Bhutto's party has not yet participated in street protests but is expected to take to the streets on Friday. However, the mayor of Rawalpindi has stated that he will prevent that march. A senior official in President Pervez Musharraf's party indicated that the state of emergency would last two or three weeks. Yet, a BBC reporter noted that Musharraf's party was sending mixed signals. Chaudhry Shujaat Hussain, president of the Pakistan Muslim League, told the Dawn newspaper: 'I am sure this situation will end within two to three weeks as President Pervez Musharraf has acknowledged the consequences of a prolonged state of emergency.' However, there are differing opinions within General Musharraf's party. Some members support holding the scheduled parliamentary elections in mid-January, while others want to postpone them. Mrs. Benazir Bhutto is currently in Islamabad discussing with other opposition leaders, although some have been arrested. The Alliance for the Restoration of Democracy, a religious political coalition, is present, while former Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif's PML-N party has refused to participate. So far, opposition parties have not mobilized their supporters for street protests, which mainly consist of lawyers and human rights activists. Hundreds of protesters have been arrested...
english_translation: The main opposition parties in Pakistan have agreed to cooperate to oppose the state of emergency declared by President Pervez Musharraf.

english_source_text: The UN Committee on the Rights of the Child urgently requested the Vatican to open its files on child sexual abusers as well as the priests and bishops who cover up their crimes. In a report released today, February 5, 2014, the Committee also criticized the Holy See for its attitude towards homosexuals, contraception, and abortion methods. The Committee called on the Vatican to reform its policies to ensure that children's rights are respected and that children have access to healthcare services. According to AFP, the UN Committee's recommendations are not binding. However, the UN can request the Vatican to implement these recommendations and report back in 2014. The Holy See has been 14 years late in submitting its most recent report. Last month, Vatican representatives were questioned for an entire day about issues related to the implementation of the UN Convention on the Rights of the Child. So far, the Holy See has often rotated the assignments of priests and bishops accused of child sexual abuse, including boys and girls, rather than reporting them to local authorities where the incidents occurred. In February 2012, Marie Collins, a victim of clerical abuse in Ireland, urged the Pope to ask victims for forgiveness as the Vatican continues to withhold records of child rape or abuse cases that occurred in dioceses in Europe and North America for decades. Nevertheless, nearing...
english_translation: The UN Committee has condemned the Vatican for child rape cases within its jurisdiction.

english_source_text: When this decision was made public, it was perhaps the first time the Bush administration had taken action to label a military branch of a sovereign nation as a terrorist organization. By designating Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a 'terrorist organization,' the Bush administration sent a clear message to governments around the world not to engage, militarily or commercially, with the IRGC. For a long time, the Bush administration had accused the elite unit of the IRGC of supplying weapons and training to militia groups in Iraq. The IRGC has also been mentioned as having assisted other organizations deemed terrorist, such as Hamas and Hezbollah. In Iran, the IRGC is a significant economic and political force. The U.S. hopes that by placing the IRGC on the list of terrorist organizations, it will cut off international funding flowing into the IRGC's projects and disrupt this branch's business plans. A U.S. official told the BBC that the decision to label the IRGC as a terrorist organization was presented to the highest levels of the U.S. power structure and would soon be announced. Additionally, through the mechanism of the UN Security Council, the Bush administration has also applied further pressure on Iran to prevent its nuclear weapons development plans.
english_translation: The U.S. has designated Iran's military as a terrorist organization.

english_source_text: On November 30, Czech news agency CTK quoted an advisor to President Vaclav Klaus stating that the President views the police intervention as "arrogant" and "ineffective." Advisor Ladislav Jakl expressed President Klaus's opinion to Nova television, indicating that Klaus believes the raid and inspection at the Vietnamese market "did not receive public applause." Klaus also expressed dissatisfaction with the Czech government's and foreign ministry's unilateral decision to suspend visa issuance for Vietnamese citizens. Jakl mentioned that President Klaus sent letters to the interior minister and the foreign minister regarding this issue. Czech media quoted police official Vladislav Husak, who stated that the intervention at the Sa Pa market was "appropriate." Criticism was directed at the actions of the Czech police after they deployed a large force into the market area on November 22 in Libus. The market management stated they do not oppose the inspection measures but disagree with the police's methods. Vietnamese students in the Czech Republic organized a petition regarding the police intervention, accusing the police of acting not only to gain points with the media but also of being offensive...
english_translation: Vietnam's president has criticized the raid and inspection at the Vietnamese market in Sa Pa.

english_source_text: The Su-30 aircraft produced by Russia (illustration) reported by local media, Major General Do Minh Tuan, Deputy Commander of the Air Defense-Air Force, stated that some ships and aircraft have been dispatched for rescue. However, he mentioned that 'there are no signs of the two pilots.' The Su-30 MK2 fighter jet formation of Regiment 923 took off on Tuesday morning, June 14, from Sao Vang Airport (Thọ Xuân - Thanh Hóa) for training exercises. One aircraft lost contact about 25 km from the mainland in Diễn Châu (Nghệ An). The military command of Nghệ An province reported that the last known position of the Su-30MK2 was to the east, about 6-7 km from Hon Mat Island, over 26 nautical miles from the mainland. Fisherman Le Van Cuong, who was fishing at sea, reported that at 8:30 AM the same day, fishermen discovered a plane crash in the sea at approximately 18-19 degrees north latitude, 106.4 degrees east longitude, about 4-6 nautical miles east of Mat Island (Cua Lo town, Nghệ An), according to Thanh Niên. It is known that the two pilots on the flight are Colonel Tran Quang Khai, Deputy Regiment Commander, Chief of Staff of Regiment 923, Division 371, and Major Nguyen Huu Cuong, Flight Leader. The Nghệ An sea area where the Su-30 MK2 is believed to have gone missing currently has three ships from Fleet 2 and ten fishing boats participating in the search for the missing aircraft. Regiment 923 has dispatched four rescue aircraft along with dozens of ships...
english_translation: A Su-30MK2 aircraft went missing in Hà Niên, with Regiment 922 reporting it to the east.

english_source_text: In a meeting on Monday, the Board of Governors consisting of 35 countries from the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA), an organization established by the UN to monitor nuclear safety, agreed to this decision. The return of international inspectors to North Korea is a crucial condition in an agreement reached last February aimed at closing the Yongbyon nuclear reactor. In exchange, Pyongyang will receive economic aid and fuel shipments. The first delivery, a shipment of fuel oil, has already left a port in the south and is expected to arrive in North Korea by the end of this week. The agreement to close the IAEA Board of Governors in a special session in Vienna, the capital of Austria, has approved sending a team of inspectors back to North Korea. The cost of this mission is about 5 million US dollars and the work will last for two years. This will be the first inspection team to return to the Yongbyon nuclear facility since UN inspectors were expelled in 2002. Diplomats in Vienna say the team could arrive in North Korea within a week to begin monitoring the closure of the nuclear facility at Yongbyon. The IAEA meeting is currently considering some findings from a three-person team led by the deputy director...
english_translation: The IAEA Board of Governors has approved the closure of the Yongbyon nuclear facility.
